guide.content0:
This page shows real-time status of GPU servers running AI models.

**One-line summary:** "Are our AI servers busy? Is there spare capacity? Do we need more?"

Monitoring targets:
- **DT Cloud (Kubernetes)** — AI models running on internal cloud
- **SSH Servers** — Directly managed GPU servers (DGX, etc.)
- **Local Servers** — Development/testing servers
guide.content1:
Kubernetes (K8s) is a system that automatically manages containers.
Think of it as a **large data center manager**.

You only need to know 3 key terms:

**Node** = 1 physical server
- Example: khdtgpuw01 = a server with 8 GPUs

**Pod** = 1 program running on a node
- Example: llm-glm-47-h200-tp8-xxx = a GLM model instance

**Cluster** = a group of nodes
- Example: DT Cloud = cluster of khdtgpuw01~05 nodes
guide.content2:
Current DT Cloud cluster GPU layout:

**Dedicated Models** — A specific model monopolizes GPUs
- GLM4.7: khdtgpuw01 (8 GPU) + khdtgpuw04 (8 GPU) = 16 GPU
- Kimi-K2.5: khdtgpuw02 (8 GPU) + khdtgpuw03 (8 GPU) = 16 GPU

TP8 = Tensor Parallel 8 — Model too large, split across 8 GPUs

**Shared Models** — Multiple models share GPUs
- khdtgpuw05: MiniMax, Qwen3-Embedding, BGE-M3, etc. 12+ models

**Other Nodes**
- khdifyw01: Redis and other infra (not AI models)
guide.content3:
**Dedicated Models**
- Monopolize GPUs, stable performance
- GPU Util accurately reflects model load
- Example: When GLM4.7 is busy, khdtgpuw01+04 GPU Util rises

**Shared Models**
- Multiple models share the same GPUs
- Hard to tell which model is causing GPU Util
- Per-model KV Cache, Running/Waiting are accurate
- Example: BGE-M3 busy causes Qwen3 GPU Util to rise too

**Card badges:**
- "8 GPU" = dedicated model, showing GPU count
- "Shared" = shared model, GPUs are shared
guide.content4:
**GPU Util (%)** = How busy the GPU chip is (nvidia-smi)

- **0~30%**: Idle — Model is idle or processing light requests
- **30~70%**: Normal — Appropriately utilized
- **70~90%**: Busy — Common during peak hours
- **90%+**: Full load — More requests may cause queuing

**Note:** GPU Util 100% isn't always bad.
Batch processing or long response generation normally reaches 100%.
The real problem is high GPU Util with **Waiting > 0**.
guide.content5:
**KV Cache (%)** = AI conversation memory usage

When LLMs generate responses, they store computation results of previous tokens in GPU memory.
This is called KV Cache.

- **0~50%**: Spare — Can handle more concurrent requests
- **50~80%**: Caution — May run out with more concurrent requests
- **80%+**: Danger — New requests force eviction of existing ones (Preemption)

High KV Cache = "Chat room is full, new guests can't enter"
Solution: Add GPUs or adjust max_num_seqs
guide.content6:
**tok/s** = Tokens processed per second

Two types:

**Prefill (Prompt) tok/s** — Speed of reading user input
- Thousands to tens of thousands tok/s is normal (parallel processing)
- Higher means shorter TTFT (Time to First Token)

**Decode (Generation) tok/s** — Speed of AI writing response
- Tens to hundreds tok/s is normal (sequential processing)
- Higher means faster response completion

**0 tok/s** = No requests right now (idle)
**null** = Not yet measured (just after server start)
guide.content7:
When a request arrives at vLLM:

[Request arrives] -> [Wait Queue (Waiting)] -> [GPU Batch (Running)] -> [Response complete]

**Running (R)** — Requests currently generating tokens on GPU
- User is receiving the response
- High is fine (just busy)

**Waiting (W)** — Requests queued, can't get on GPU
- User is stuck at "Loading..."
- **Even 1 means someone is waiting**
- **5+ is serious overload**

Persistent Waiting -> Need GPU scaling or model optimization
guide.content8:
**Preemption** = Running request forcibly stopped due to KV Cache shortage

Sequence:
1. KV Cache reaches 100%
2. New request arrives
3. vLLM forcibly **stops (Preempts)** an existing request
4. Stopped request is reprocessed from scratch later

User experience: "Response cut off and restarted" or "Extremely slow response"

**Preemption > 0 is a confirmed GPU memory shortage signal.**
Solution: Adjust gpu_memory_utilization, reduce max_num_seqs, or add GPUs
guide.content9:
How data reaches the screen:

**DT Cloud (Kubernetes)**
vLLM Pod -> Prometheus (collect/store) -> Our API (60s polling) -> DB -> UI

- Prometheus periodically scrapes vLLM /metrics endpoint
- Our API calls Prometheus API to fetch data
- tok/s is calculated from counter delta (cumulative value changes)

**SSH Servers (Direct Management)**
SSH connection -> nvidia-smi + vLLM /metrics direct collection -> DB -> UI

- Connects via SSH to read GPU status and vLLM metrics directly
- Works independently without Prometheus
guide.content10:
**Immediate action needed:**
- Waiting sustained 5+ -> Severe user response delays
- Preemption occurring -> KV Cache shortage, forced request termination
- KV Cache 90%+ -> Preemption will start soon

**Watch carefully:**
- KV Cache 50~80% -> Possible issues at peak time
- GPU Util 90%+ & Waiting > 0 -> Approaching capacity limit

**Normal:**
- GPU Util high but Waiting = 0 -> Processing well
- tok/s = 0 -> No requests (night/weekend)
- KV Cache 0% -> Idle state (normal)

**Tip:** Check peak time (14~16h) patterns in the 30-day analysis heatmap.

prediction.title: GPU Demand Prediction
prediction.refreshBenchmarksComplete: Benchmark refresh complete. Refresh the page to see changes.
prediction.analysisInProgress: Analysis in progress. Will auto-update when complete.
prediction.peakKvTooltip:
KV Cache = Memory used by AI models to remember conversation context.

Above 80%, memory shortage may cause slower responses or request queuing.
Users will experience degraded response speed.
prediction.waitingFrequencyTooltip:
Waiting requests = Percentage of requests waiting because GPU is busy.

Above 30%, users will notice longer response wait times.
This is a direct signal that GPU scaling is needed.
prediction.peakShortVramTooltip:
VRAM = GPU working memory.

If this number is above 0, current GPU memory is insufficient
and service quality may degrade during peak hours.
prediction.immediateNeedTooltip:
B300 = NVIDIA's latest GPU equipment (192GB memory).

Number of GPU units that need to be added immediately
to maintain service quality at current peak load.
prediction.currentVramTooltip:
Total GPU memory capacity currently available.
Sum of all server GPU VRAM.
prediction.requiredVramTooltip:
Total GPU memory capacity needed to serve
the target number of users.
Includes 1.5x safety margin.
prediction.additionalNeededTooltip:
Number of B300 GPU units to purchase
to achieve the target user count.

Current infra + this quantity = target service capability
prediction.saturationTooltip:
Estimated time until GPU capacity reaches its limit
if current growth trends continue.

GPU expansion must be completed before this point
to prevent service outages.
prediction.scalingRatioTooltip:
Expansion ratio needed from current to target.

Reflects both user count growth and
per-user token consumption growth.
prediction.bottleneckDimTooltip: Bottleneck dimension: the most constrained of 3 resources
prediction.tokenGrowthPerUserTooltip:
Weekly growth rate of per-user token consumption.

High values mean GPU load increases rapidly
even with the same number of users.
Surges with agentic AI, coding tools, etc.
prediction.tokenGrowth6moTooltip:
Per-user token growth rate compounded over 6 months (26 weeks).

Example: 5% weekly growth -> ~3.6x after 6 months
This multiplier directly affects scaling calculations.
prediction.errorRateTooltip:
Percentage of errors (HTTP 400+) among all requests in the last 7 days.

Above 5% signals GPU overload or service issues.
Higher error rates add safety margin to predictions.
prediction.unmonitoredEquipDesc: (No monitoring data, estimated using avg utilization)
prediction.unmonitoredTooltip:
Unmonitored equipment: No monitoring data
Estimated using average utilization of connected equipment
prediction.calcDauNote: DAU growth already included in target. Only per-user token growth added.
prediction.monthlyForecast: Monthly GPU Demand Forecast (to year-end, with per-user token growth)
prediction.tokenGrowthColTooltip: Per-user token consumption growth rate compounded to this month.
prediction.growthOnlyB300Tooltip:
Additional B300 needed just for token growth with current infra.
Independent of target users — pure growth response cost.
prediction.targetB300Tooltip: Total additional B300 needed for target users + token growth.
prediction.requiredVramColTooltip: Predicted total GPU memory (VRAM) needed for this month's target.
prediction.safetyNote: * Includes 1.5x safety margin and error correction. Only per-user token growth reflected (DAU growth already in target).
prediction.kvCacheTooltipShort:
KV Cache above 80% means memory shortage — new requests may be queued or responses slowed.
Consider GPU scaling immediately.
prediction.waitingTooltipShort:
Waiting requests occurring in 30%+ of monitoring.
Users are likely experiencing response delays.
prediction.preemptionTooltipShort:
Preemption = running requests being evicted.
Frequent occurrences cause response failures or delays.

kpi.compositeDesc: Composite capacity = max(Throughput%, KV Memory%, Concurrency%) — vs benchmark (observed P95 peak) | Business hours: KST 9-18

analysis.heatmapTitle: 3 Key Metric Heatmap (Date x Hour, 30 days)
analysis.heatmapTpsDesc: Tokens generated per second — how fast AI is responding. Higher = more active.
analysis.heatmapKvDesc: AI conversation memory usage — above 80% may cause slower responses or request queuing.
analysis.heatmapWaitDesc: Requests waiting because GPU is busy — 1+ means users are waiting. 5+ is serious.
analysis.heatmapPreemptDesc: Requests evicted due to VRAM shortage — running requests forcibly stopped. Users experience response failures.
analysis.heatmapTpsPctDesc: Current processing speed vs benchmark (past peak). 100% = past peak level. Above = all-time highest load.
analysis.heatmapKvPctDesc: KV Cache usage (0-100%). 80%+ red = memory shortage risk.
analysis.heatmapConcPctDesc: Concurrent requests vs benchmark. Above 100% = more simultaneous requests than past peak. Overload signal.
analysis.heatmapGpuDesc: nvidia-smi GPU utilization — how busy the GPU chip is. 100% isn't always overload (model loading alone can be high).

step0.title: Let's register an LLM model
step0.desc1: This guide walks you through the entire process of registering a new LLM model.
step0.bullet1: The model creation dialog will open automatically
step0.bullet2: We'll tell you what to enter in each field
step0.bullet3: We'll guide you through testing and saving
step0.bullet4: After completion, we'll show you how to test with curl
step0.tip: If you already have a registered model, you can click the clone icon in the model list to copy existing settings and just change the API key for a quick setup.

step1.title: 1. Select a model type
step1.desc: Choose one of the type buttons at the top of the modal.
step1.chatDesc: Text conversation (GPT-4o, Claude, etc.). The most common type.
step1.tip: If this is your first time, select CHAT. Most LLM APIs use this type.

step2.title: 2. Enter basic information
step2.modelIdDesc: Unique identifier used internally by the system
step2.endpointUrlDesc: Base URL of the LLM API (without path)
step2.tip: Paths like /chat/completions, /embeddings are automatically appended based on the model type. Just enter the base URL.
step2.warning: Model ID cannot be changed after registration. Choose carefully.

step3.title: 3. Set API key and options
step3.maxTokensDesc: Maximum tokens the model can process
step3.tip: Extra Headers and Extra Body are only needed for custom authentication. Leave them empty in most cases.

step4.title: 4. Test the endpoint
step4.desc: Click the 'Run Endpoint Test' button at the bottom of the modal.
step4.minPass: At least 2 out of 5 must pass to save.
step4.warningUrl: Verify the endpoint URL is correct
step4.warningApiKey: Verify the API key is valid
step4.warningToolCall: Verify the model supports tool calls
step4.tip: IMAGE, EMBEDDING, RERANKING, and ASR models run type-specific tests.

step5.title: 5. Vision and additional options
step5.desc: For CHAT models, you can set additional options.
step5.visionDesc: Enable if the model can process image inputs.
step5.visionHint: Most recent models like GPT-4o, Claude 3.5 support this.
step5.activeDesc: Disabling hides the model from the list. Use for maintenance.
step5.sortOrderDesc: Display order in the list (lower numbers appear first)
step5.tip: Enabling Vision requires passing an additional Vision test.

step6.title: 6. Save
step6.desc: Once all required fields are filled and tests pass, click the 'Save' button at the bottom of the modal.
step6.requiredField: Check that no fields are empty
step6.retryInfo: Review the information and click 'Save' again
step6.checkId: Model ID, Display Name, Endpoint URL entered
step6.tip: After saving, check the health check status in the model list. A green dot means it's healthy.

step7.title: Model registered!
step7.tokenHint: Copy the token from Browser DevTools → Application → localStorage → agent_stats_token.
step7.nextStep1: Verify the health check green light in the model list
step7.nextStep2: Create a service in Service Management and link this model
step7.nextStep3: Test with the proxy curl above after linking
step7.tip: To use a different API key with the same endpoint, click the Clone button on this model in the model list.

step8.title: Verify your model
step8.desc: The {{modelName}} model you just registered is highlighted in the list.
step8.healthcheckDesc: A green dot on the left of the model card means it's healthy. Auto-checked every 10 minutes.
step8.cloneDesc: Click the 'Clone' button on the model card to copy settings and quickly add with just an API key change.
step8.testDesc: Click the play button (▶) to run a manual health check anytime.
step8.tip: Now create a service in Service Management and link this model to call it through the proxy.

step0.title: What is Saved M/M?
step0.desc: Saved Man-Month measures the workforce savings achieved by adopting AI services.
step0.manageTitle: What you manage on this page:
step0.manage1: 📊 Saved M/M by service — Overview of savings across all services
step0.manage2: 🏢 Department Saved M/M input — Enter values per department in the bottom tab
step0.manage3: 🤖 AI estimates — Auto-estimated for services without manual input
step0.tip: The top table shows the service list and summary. The bottom 'Department Saved M/M Input' tab is where you enter your department's M/M values.

step1.title: 1. Understanding the service list
step1.desc: The top table shows the Saved M/M status of all services.
step1.mauDesc: Monthly active user count. Indicates service usage scale.
step1.savedMMDesc: Actual savings aggregated from department inputs. Hover to see breakdown by department.
step1.aiEstDesc: AI auto-estimates for services without department input, based on DAU and usage patterns.
step1.tip: Click column headers to sort. Click a service name to go to its detail page.

step2.title: 2. Entering department Saved M/M
step2.desc1: Check the 'Department Saved M/M Input' tab at the bottom of the page.
step2.desc2: Services used by your department are listed here.
step2.inputStep1: Click the edit icon (pencil) on the service row
step2.inputStep2: Enter the Saved M/M value as a number (e.g. 2.5)
step2.warning: M/M values are monthly. 1 M/M = one person working for one month. 0.5 M/M = about half a month of work saved.

step3.title: 3. Understanding AI estimates
step3.desc: Services without manual M/M input will automatically show AI estimates.
step3.tip: Click the expand arrow next to the AI estimate to see the estimation rationale.

step4.title: 4. Tips for effective M/M management
step4.tip1Title: 📅 Enter last month's results at the beginning of each month
step4.tip1Desc: Data is managed on a monthly basis. The current aggregation month is shown at the top of the table.
step4.tip2Desc: e.g. 'Code review automation saved 1 reviewer's effort' — specifying which tasks were saved helps with reporting.
step4.tip3Desc: Use AI estimates as a reference before entering your values. You can override them with your own numbers.
step4.tip4Desc: Compare per-service M/M goals and actual results in the 'Goal' column of the top table.

step5.title: Guide complete!
step5.desc: You now know how to manage Saved M/M.
step5.summary1: Review overall status in the top table
step5.summary2: Enter your department's results in the 'Department Saved M/M Input' tab
step5.summary3: Use AI estimates as a reference
step5.tip: You can reopen this guide anytime.

intro.title: Let's register a service
intro.desc: Registering a service lets you link LLM models, track usage, and manage team members.
intro.afterTitle: After registering a service, you can:
intro.modelLink: 🤖 Model linking — LLM model connection + Round Robin + Fallback
intro.tip: Click Next to open the service registration wizard.

wizard0.title: 1. Enter basic information
wizard0.desc: This is the first step of the wizard.
wizard0.serviceCodeDesc: Internal identifier. Only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens
wizard0.warning: Service code cannot be changed after registration! It's used as the x-service-id header in curl calls.
wizard0.tip: After entering, click 'Next' to advance both the wizard and guide together.

wizard1.title: 2. Select service type and category
wizard1.standardDesc: Standard — Interactive services with UI (chatbot, search, etc.)
wizard1.apiOnlyDesc: Services that send usage records through their own API without going through the proxy. Usually leave this off.
wizard1.categoryDesc: Select one or more categories for the service.
wizard1.tip: If unsure, select Standard type with the closest category. You can change it later.

wizard2.title: 3. Set links (optional)
wizard2.desc: All items below are optional. You can leave them empty and add later.
wizard2.logoUrlDesc: Logo image for the service card

wizard3.title: 4. Review and register
wizard3.desc: Review the information. Click the guide's 'Register Service' button to register.
wizard3.formatError: Service code: only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens
wizard3.retryInfo: Go back with 'Previous', fix the issue, and try again
wizard3.checkCode: Service code and display name entered

success.title: Service registered!
success.moveDesc: Click 'Go to Detail Page' to see the service detail page with highlighted tabs.
success.tip: The service detail page has Dashboard, Model Management, Member Management, Rate Limit tabs.

step0.title: 📊 Dashboard
step0.activeUsers: Active Users — Users in the last 30 minutes
step0.tip: The highlighted 'Dashboard' tab is the one you're currently viewing.

step1.title: 🤖 Model Management
step1.desc: The core tab for linking LLM models to the service.
step1.aliasDesc: Register multiple models under one alias for automatic Round Robin distribution.
step1.fallbackDesc: Automatically switches to a backup model on primary failure. Configurable retry count.
step1.weightDesc: Use +/- buttons to adjust per-model traffic ratio.
step1.tip: Enter an alias name in the 'Add new model alias' area and add models.

step2.title: 👥 Member Management
step2.desc: Manage team members who can access the service.
step2.searchHint: Use the search at the top to find and add users.

step3.title: ⚡ Rate Limit
step3.commonLimitDesc: Applied equally to all users. e.g. 100,000 tokens / 24 hours
step3.perUserLimitDesc: Assign separate limits to specific users. Takes priority over common limit.
step3.tip: Time window can be set to 5 hours or 24 hours.

step4.title: 📋 Proxy Call Test
step4.desc: After linking a model, test with the curl below.
step4.curlHint: Set x-service-id to the service code, x-user-id to the user ID, and model to the model alias. Department info is auto-registered from Knox on first call.

step5.title: Guide complete!
step5.desc: You've reviewed all major features of the service detail page.
step5.summary1: Model Management tab — Link LLM models (RR + Fallback)
step5.summary2: Member Management tab — Add team members / set roles
step5.tip: Click each tab to explore freely.

businessDayToggle.includeTitle: Showing all dates (click to exclude weekends/holidays)

hero.title: Agent Registry & Dashboard
hero.subtitle: Unified control tower for enterprise AI services

architecture.deployNote: Blue-Green deployment via Docker Compose · git pull && ./deploy.sh

footer.quote: "Empowering every team to harness the power of AI"
footer.author: — Agent Registry Team

features.0: LLM Proxy (Chat · Embedding · Rerank · Image · ASR)
features.1: Weighted round-robin load balancing & SubModel failover
features.2: API-Only services — external usage tracking (proxy bypass)
features.4: Service marketplace & deployment scope management (all · BU · team)
features.5: Public dashboard & admin permission request workflow
features.6: User management Excel export (monthly tabs + Codemate external API integration)

features.0: SSH-based real-time GPU monitoring & 3-tier throughput analysis (theoretical · peak · current)
features.1: Model-centric resource monitor UI (K8s node → per-model grouping, dedicated/shared/SSH 3-class)
features.2: ModelGroupCard: 24h charts, debug logs, per-node GPU details
features.3: vLLM metrics normalization — counter delta approach (rate() counter reset fix)
features.4: Unified dashboard with 9 tabs (usage · services · DAU/MAU · M/M · dept · BU · analytics · latency · GPU)
features.5: Redis read-through cache (28 endpoints) + 15-30s precompute warming + write buffering bulk INSERT
features.6: Model latency & health check (10-min auto probing) + FP8 precision auto-detection
features.7: LLM model heatmap — per-model date x hour call/response time/error analysis (6 tabs)
features.8: Error tracking & failover attempt detail logs + error rate trend charts
features.10: Service quality metrics: 6 types (TTFT · TPOT · E2E · KV Cache · Preemption · Queue)
features.12: GPU realtime precompute (15s background worker, API response <5ms)
features.13: Analytics: single query → N-combination memory slicing → instant dropdown response
features.14: LLM heatmap precompute (6 queries once → all-model slicing) + Batch API (N calls → 1)
features.15: Page Visibility polling pause + updatedAt comparison re-render prevention
features.16: Auto-merge identical models (endpoint+name, CTE SQL) + per-service aliasName group charts

features.1: 5-level model visibility (PUBLIC · BUSINESS_UNIT · TEAM · ADMIN · SUPER_ADMIN)
features.2: OIDC authentication — Open WebUI/ADK/LangChain per-user tracking + SSO direct
features.3: OIDC client management UI (Super Admin only)
features.4: Audit logs & request logs — full admin action tracking
features.5: Knox employee authentication & org chart sync (incl. overseas R&D centers)
features.7: Internal API Swagger + external integration API auto-generated docs

features.1: Dev/QA server (port 8095, shared DB + Redis DB 15 cache isolation)
features.2: Auto-block DB schema changes (Dev) + migration approval process
features.3: Docker Compose multi-service (PostgreSQL · Redis · Nginx · API · Dashboard)

0.title: Day 1 — Project Kickoff & Foundation
0.desc: 45+ commits in a single day. Multi-service dashboard initial architecture, LLM proxy routing, service/model CRUD, 5-tier permission system, legacy data migration (battle with PostgreSQL UUID casting), unified dashboard, department usage charts, model rating system. First rebrand from Nexus Coder → AX Portal.

1.title: Stabilization & Monitoring Foundation
1.desc: Added LLM latency monitoring, model drag-and-drop sorting, docs-site brand landing page redesign, service filtering, dev environment (hot-reload), express-rate-limit integration, user info in proxy logs, error retry logic.

2.title: Holiday Management & Service Enhancement
2.desc: Holiday management + business-day DAU analysis, model visibility (BU/team restriction), service deletion/data reset. AIPO → ONCE → multiple rebranding attempts. Countless UI overflow/clipping bug battles.

3.title: SubModel & Failover System
3.desc: SubModel load balancing (Redis-based round-robin), auto failover, endpoint health checks (Chat + Tool Call tests), extraHeaders support, context window overflow auto-recovery, LLM quality test auto-scheduler.

4.title: Infrastructure Hardening & Error Telemetry
4.desc: Vision (VL) model support, high-concurrency stabilization, real-time DAU tracking, error telemetry system (bulk delete / JSON copy), OpenAI standard error format compatibility.

5.title: docs-site Full React Redesign
5.desc: Complete docs-site rebuild from VitePress → React + Vite + Tailwind. Logo/favicon replacement, Nexus Bot renaming, per-service guide documentation.

6.title: v2 Full Refactoring — Major Redesign
6.desc: 30+ commits in a single day. Port scheme change (8090/8091/8092), container renaming, collapsible sidebar, service marketplace, rate limiting (per-service + per-user), premium UI/UX full redesign (Linear/Vercel-grade), unified user management, Swagger UI intranet compatibility (CDN blocking workaround), Embedding · Rerank · Image proxy extension, 1000-user concurrent optimization.

7.title: Enterprise Feature Explosion
7.desc: Public API design, service deployment scope (all/BU/team), model copy feature, Samsung Knox employee auth integration, public dashboard, Blue-Green deploy script stabilization. Agent Stats → Agent Registry final rebrand.

8.title: AI Features & Org Integration Surge
8.desc: ASR model proxy, org hierarchy (team/center) info, Saved M/M management, AI M/M auto-estimation (5-business-day average), admin permission request/approval workflow, API-Only service (external usage tracking), error management + AI auto-analysis, service logo AI generation (IMAGE model), API password management, audit log localization. 40+ commits in a day.

9.title: Insights & Department Analytics Framework
9.desc: Per-department Saved M/M management, AI usage rate insights (center → team → service drill-down), service usage insights, center grouping logic fix (10+ commits of debugging), owner transfer feature, DailyUsageStat legacy complete removal.

10.title: Major Stabilization & Feature Completion
10.desc: 60+ commits marathon in a day. Weekend/holiday toggle, all model type health checks (ASR/IMAGE/ComfyUI), response latency trends (daily/weekly/monthly), service error management tab, model fallback chain, health check Blue-Green dedup (Redis distributed lock), daily error rate trends, chunk 404 auto-recovery (lazyWithRetry). Health check timeout adjusted 3 times (10min → 30min → 9min30s).

11.title: GPU Monitoring & AI Feature Focus
11.desc: SSH-based real-time GPU monitoring, LLM serving metrics auto-detection (vLLM/SGLang/Ollama/TGI), 3-tier throughput analysis (theoretical · peak · current), GPU demand forecasting, per-role onboarding guide tour, registration wizard, public dashboard per-team charts.

12.title: AI Assistant Chatbot & Final Enhancements
12.desc: AI assistant chatbot (SSE streaming, role-based dynamic prompts, auto page navigation), 382 → 6 query optimization + Batch API, AI coaching reports, 6 service quality metrics (TTFT/TPOT/E2E/KV Cache/Preemption/Queue), FP8 auto-detection. Overcame Prisma napi limits — stabilized GPU snapshot bulk queries via direct node-postgres connection. GPU demand forecasting full redesign (measured B300 calculation + IDC/Deloitte/Gartner industry trend integration + auto executive report generation). Platform Story page.
12.tags: AI Chatbot,Performance,napi Overcome,GPU Forecast Redesign,Platform Story

13.title: Permission System Hardening & GPU Power Hourly Transition
13.desc: SUPER_ADMIN role change button visible only to super admins. Hardcoded Super Admin fallback restored (DB-first, fallback to existing). TEAM visibility ancestor matching fix. GPU power usage collection switched from daily (date) to hourly (timestamp) + existing data preservation migration. Swagger docs updated.

14.title: Prometheus Integration & DTGPT Connection
14.desc: DTGPT K8s cluster (5 nodes, 40x H200) Prometheus auto-collector — works immediately on deploy. 14-day vLLM data backfill (KV cache old/new name mapping + preemption). DTGPT replica 1:N mapping. Unconnected equipment (HPC 54x H200) registration and estimation included. GPU power usage hourly transition. Estimation conditions notice banner (editable, JIRA link).

15.title: GPU Benchmark-Based Capacity System Full Redesign
15.desc: Switched from compute-bound theoretical max (0.8%) to per-server observed P95 peak benchmark. Established 3-metric framework: tok/s + KV Cache% + queue count. Composite capacity = max(3D) — a 0-100% metric matching real-world perception. GPU deficit calculated in 3D (throughput/KV memory/concurrency each as B300, bottleneck identification). 8 heatmaps (date x hour, 30 days): tok/s, KV%, queue, preemption, throughput%, KV%, concurrency%, GPU util%. Peak (14-16h) / off-hours (20-06h) / overall average cards. Prisma napi limitation → fundamentally resolved via direct node-postgres. Benchmark auto-calculation (P95) + manual override + daily cron refresh.

16.title: Dev/QA Server & Redis Caching Optimization
16.desc: Introduced Dev/QA server for pre-deployment validation (port 8095, fully isolated from production). deploy.sh dev/dev-stop/dev-status commands. Auto-block DB schema changes (prisma db push blocked + detection warning → syngha.han approval then migrate). Redis DB 15 cache isolation. --no-deps to protect production infrastructure. Redis caching optimization: withCache fail-open generic cache utility, incrementUsage 12 calls → 1 pipeline (92% RTT reduction), read-through cache on 12 admin/user/public stats endpoints (60-300s TTL), my-usage 3-query parallelization.

17.title: Full Endpoint Caching + Precompute Warming + Real-time Refresh UI
17.desc: Applied withCache read-through cache to 22 uncached stats endpoints (total 28). Introduced 60s cache warming service — background precomputation of dashboard batch core endpoints (by-service/by-dept/error-rate/health-status etc.) stored in Redis. Thundering herd completely eliminated: first user gets cache hit, DB load constant regardless of user count. Frontend data refresh timestamp display + manual refresh button.
17.tags: withCache Full Coverage,Precompute Warming,Thundering Herd Fix,Refresh Time UI

18.title: 300 Concurrent Users — Write Buffering + Connection Pool + TTL Reduction
18.desc: DGX H200-targeted 3000 DAU / 300 concurrent user optimization. usage_logs/request_logs write buffering (1s interval bulk INSERT, 90% DB load reduction vs individual INSERTs). Connection pool expansion (pgPool 10 → 50, Prisma connection_limit sync). Cache TTL across-the-board reduction (120 → 60, 300 → 90, 2x real-time responsiveness). Precompute cycle 15s / warming cycle 30s. Warming targets expanded 3 → 7 (4 by-dept endpoints added). Graceful shutdown with remaining buffer flush guarantee.
18.tags: Write Buffering,Bulk INSERT,Connection Pool,TTL Reduction,DGX Optimization

19.title: Full-Stack Optimization — Query/Cache/Frontend/Infrastructure
19.desc: User management _count:usageLogs subquery removal (50x COUNT → requestCount aggregation), admin duplicate query removal, filter options parallelization. Cache invalidation pattern (invalidateCache — instant related cache deletion on write). Metadata endpoint withCache (scope BU/dept/org-tree 300s). Morgan double-logging removal (50-100ms per request). Proxy rate limit parallel lookup (40 → 20ms). Unnecessary DB re-query removal. PostgreSQL WAL/checkpoint tuning (shared_buffers 8GB, wal_buffers 64MB, checkpoint 15min). Frontend phase parallelization (sequential 3s → parallel 0.5s), StatCard memo, unused chart.js bundle removal (50KB reduction).
19.tags: Query Optimization,Cache Invalidation,Morgan Removal,WAL Tuning,Phase Parallelization,Memo

20.title: Excel Export & Codemate External API Integration
20.desc: Added full Excel export for user management. Monthly tab separation — service usage aggregated per month in separate sheets. Codemate with Roo / Codemate external API data integrated into Excel export for a complete view of all AI service usage at a glance.

21.title: LLM Model Heatmap & SSO Direct Debugging
21.desc: Super admin exclusive LLM model heatmap page — per-model date x hour call pattern analysis. Card-style model selector (call count bar, real-time indicator), 6-tab heatmap (call count/avg response time/P95/timeout/error rate/success rate), daily trend bar chart. SSO direct integration stabilization — domain typo fix (secsso → secso), CLIENT_ID replacement, sid cookie forwarding, redirect_uri exact match — 6 consecutive debugging sessions to fully stabilize Samsung SSO authentication.

22.title: OIDC Auth System — Per-User Usage Auto-Tracking
22.desc: Full OIDC (OpenID Connect) authentication system built for automatic per-user usage tracking from external services like Open WebUI, Google ADK, and LangChain. Auth Server (HTTPS :9050) — OIDC Provider (authorize/token/userinfo) + Mock SSO (dev). Samsung SSO direct integration (form_post, RS256 cert verification, genai.samsungds.net intermediary removal). Gateway body.user support — user identification via OpenAI standard user field (replacing x-user-id header). Python SDK (agent_platform_auth.py) — single setup_auth() call for auto user injection across OpenAI/LangChain/ADK (OpenAI SDK monkey-patch + LiteLLM callback dual coverage). Dashboard OIDC login mode added (existing SSO backward compatible). Open WebUI Playwright E2E verification complete. Google ADK 1.28 + 2.0 Alpha compatibility verified. 8 security review fixes (SSRF blocking, client secret frontend removal, open redirect prevention, etc.). docs-site 5-page guide + Agent prompt + example zip. deploy.sh SSL auto-generation + OIDC_ISSUER auto-detection.
22.tags: OIDC,Auth Server,body.user,Open WebUI,ADK,LangChain,SSO Direct,Python SDK,Security Review

23.title: vLLM Metrics Normalization & Model-Centric Monitoring UI
23.desc: vLLM --disable-log-stats issue analysis and DT Cloud response rationale. Discovered Prometheus rate() recording rule counter reset issue → switched to counter delta approach. Resource monitor model-centric UI (K8s node → per-model grouping, dedicated/shared/SSH 3-class). ModelGroupCard: 24h charts, debug logs, per-node GPU details. GPU monitoring guidebook for beginners (interactive slides).

24.title: KPI 3-Class System & 3D Analysis Enhancement
24.desc: GPU KPIs split into SSH/DT-dedicated/DT-shared 3-class (accurate capacity per operating environment). KPI 3D decomposition (throughput%/KV memory%/concurrency%) — independent monitoring of each bottleneck metric. Heatmap per-model dropdown for multi-node analysis. Business-day averages also applied to 3-class. KV Cache 0-1 → % conversion normalized. Unified dashboard identical model aggregation (endpoint+name basis). Kimi-K2 model parameter mapping added.

25.title: Full Precompute Architecture — GPU · Analytics · LLM Heatmap Unified Performance Optimization
25.desc: GPU Realtime precompute (15s cycle) — benchmark/peak TPS calculation offloaded to background worker, API response from hundreds of ms to <5ms. GPU Analytics precompute (5min cycle) — single full query, then memory slicing for all frontend dropdown combinations into Redis batch cache (0 additional DB queries). LLM Heatmap precompute (5min cycle) — 6 queries (usage_logs/request_logs/health_check_logs x hourly/daily) run once, then all-model memory slicing → Redis pipeline batch cache. Batch endpoint (GET /model-heatmap/all) — Redis pipeline for all-model cache bulk read, frontend N API calls → unified to 1. Instant cache invalidation on server CRUD. Page Visibility API to completely stop polling on inactive tabs. updatedAt comparison to prevent chart re-render when GPU data unchanged. Analytics/heatmap frontend prefetch for 0ms loading on tab/model switch.

26.title: Dashboard Identical Model Aggregation & Per-Service Alias Grouping
26.desc: Unified dashboard: auto-aggregation of multiple registered models with same endpoint+modelName (API key/header variations) — using sortOrder-minimum model's displayName as representative name, aggregation details visible via banner/badge. Service dashboard: all charts aggregated by service-registered aliasName (display name) — per-model cumulative usage, per-user cumulative usage, usage analysis all in alias group units. DB source and LLM management pages unchanged — operates only at chart aggregation layer.

27.title: Promotional Model Management & Docs-site Landing Page Model Showcase
27.desc: Feature to promote available models for official AX project registration. Super admin can select/deselect promotional models in real-time → instantly reflected on docs-site landing page. Model table promoted field + public API (/public/promoted-models, no auth required) + admin toggle API (with audit log). Docs-site glassmorphism card grid showing model type, Vision support, and Max Tokens info.

28.title: Full i18n Support — Korean/English Across All Pages
28.desc: Built multi-language infrastructure using react-i18next. 42 translation sections with 2,462 translation keys covering all 47 files (31 pages + 16 components) with complete Korean/English support. Real-time language switching via sidebar toggle with localStorage persistence. Tour guides, chart components, and AI assistant chatbot all fully translated.
